---
title: "timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-12
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
tbl-colwidths: [30, 22, 22, 26]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![2×3 grid of day-10-vs-day-0 amino-acid Fisher volcano panels: rows are conditions (top = BWM, bottom = control); columns are ribosome sites (A / P / E). Each panel plots −log₁₀(FDR) against log₂(odds ratio); triangles mark FDR<0.05 hits and circles are non-significant. Glyphs are coloured by amino-acid property (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple). Control,A and control,P are the most populated panels (8–9 FDR<0.05 hits each); BWM,A,N sits as a far-right enriched outlier (log₂(OR) ≈ +0.83) and BWM,E,K sits as a far-left depleted outlier (log₂(OR) ≈ −0.34) at the top of their panels.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/composite/aa_fisher_composite.png)

Each glyph is a single amino acid at one (condition, site) BH-FDR family of 20; horizontal dashed line is FDR = 0.05 and vertical dashed lines mark ±0.5 log₂(OR). Positive log₂(OR) means day-10-enriched relative to day-0 *within the same condition*; negative means day-10-depleted relative to day-0. The test does not compare BWM to control directly — that contrast is implied by reading paired top/bottom rows of the grid side-by-side.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`
- **Sister CSV(s):** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv`, `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv`, `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon.csv`, `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`, `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), day-10-vs-day-0 2×2 contingency per (condition, site, aa); BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 AAs (source: user-confirmed)
- **Design:** Per (condition, site, aa) 2×2 day-10-vs-day-0 contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact within one condition; replicates summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs (120 tests total). The test is **within-condition**: BWM and control are tested separately and never share a contingency.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition, timepoint) are summed into the 2×2 before Fisher's exact; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled (day_10 + day_0) totals at the condition level are in the thousands; Fisher returns vanishing p-values for tiny absolute deviations, so `odds_ratio` (log-axis) is the primary effect column, not p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(condition,site)** — BH-FDR is computed independently within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs; `p_adj` means "corrected within this sub-family", not across the full 120-row file.
- **within-condition-clean** — unlike the between-condition or between-timepoint Wilcoxon families, no condition pooling and no timepoint pooling cross-contaminate the test; the design is structurally cleaner.

---

## Headline

**Strong signal at AA-level within-condition Fisher d10 vs d0: 35/120 hits at FDR<0.05 (12/60 BWM, 23/60 control). BWM is dominated by site-A direction shifts (N enriched OR=1.78; L, Y, A depleted) plus site-E K↓/Q↑/C↑ and site-P K↓/E↑. Control is dominated by broad site-P shifts (G, C, R, E enriched; M, W, Q, K, I, L depleted) and site-A G/R↑ vs Q/W/L↓. Three largest-magnitude BWM-vs-control direction-divergent cells: site-A N (BWM↑1.78 vs control flat 0.86, p_adj=0.053), site-E K (BWM↓0.79 vs control↑1.13, both FDR<0.01), and site-P G (BWM flat 1.01 vs control↑1.28, p_adj=5.7e-8). Largest-magnitude shared-direction cells: site-P E both enriched (BWM↑1.32 FDR<1e-3 / control↑1.16 FDR<0.01), and site-P K both depleted (BWM↓0.76 FDR<5e-5 / control,P,K within the broad site-P depletion signature M/W/Q/K/I/L↓).**

## Top hits

The effect column is `odds_ratio`. Direction is fixed by the day-10-vs-day-0 contingency layout *within one condition*: OR > 1 = day-10-enriched relative to day-0 in that condition, OR < 1 = day-10-depleted. `p_adj` is BH-corrected within each (condition, site) family of 20 AAs. Sub-tables below are split per-direction within each (condition, site) Dylan-group, ordered A / P / E (ribostall ribosome-site canonical ordering). Each row's amino-acid one-letter code is expanded to `<letter> (<full name>)` on first appearance within each sub-table.

### BWM, A site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| N (asparagine) | 1.777 | 9.17e-07 |  |
| F (phenylalanine) | 1.300 | 0.0264 |  |

### BWM, A site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L (leucine) | 0.733 | 1.36e-04 |  |
| Y (tyrosine) | 0.731 | 0.00258 |  |
| A (alanine) | 0.783 | 0.0178 |  |

### BWM, P site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| E (glutamate) | 1.322 | 2.44e-04 |  |

### BWM, P site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K (lysine) | 0.757 | 5.51e-05 |  |

### BWM, E site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| C (cysteine) | 1.788 | 0.0267 | rare-aa-low-count |
| Q (glutamine) | 1.426 | 0.00145 |  |

### BWM, E site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K (lysine) | 0.790 | 4.52e-05 |  |

### control, A site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| G (glycine) | 1.288 | 9.89e-08 |  |
| R (arginine) | 1.261 | 2.13e-05 |  |

### control, A site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| W (tryptophan) | 0.658 | 0.00216 | rare-aa-low-count |
| Q (glutamine) | 0.657 | 9.89e-08 |  |

### control, P site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| G (glycine) | 1.285 | 5.67e-08 |  |
| C (cysteine) | 1.266 | 0.0493 |  |
| R (arginine) | 1.168 | 0.00668 |  |

### control, P site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| M (methionine) | 0.624 | 1.08e-05 | rare-aa-low-count |
| W (tryptophan) | 0.616 | 0.0137 | rare-aa-low-count |

### control, E site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

*(Dylan's Top hits table did not include enriched rows for control,E; the corresponding volcano plot below has 1 FDR<0.05 enriched hit (K, log₂(OR) ≈ +0.18) outside Dylan's curated top-hits list.)*

### control, E site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| amino acid | effect (`odds_ratio`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| W (tryptophan) | 0.682 | 0.0424 | rare-aa-low-count |

### Flag glossary

- `rare-aa-low-count` — at least one timepoint cell for this (condition, site, aa) row has `day_X_count` below the test's stability threshold (typically < 100 for BWM, < 200 for control). OR estimates and CIs are unstable at small k; magnitude weight should account for the count rather than rank by `odds_ratio` alone.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 120 (60 per condition)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 35 (BWM 12, control 23)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 39 (BWM 13, control 26)
- `min adjusted-p`: 9.89e-08 (tied: control,A,G and control,A,Q)
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher with pooled N in the thousands has no meaningful floor; the dominant statistical-design concern is `large-N-Fisher-anticonservative` (family-wide), not floor.
- Per (condition, site):
  - BWM,A: 6 sig at FDR<0.05 (N, L, Y, A, I, F); min p_adj = 9.17e-07
  - BWM,E: 4 sig (K, Q, S, C); min p_adj = 4.52e-05
  - BWM,P: 2 sig (K, E); min p_adj = 5.51e-05
  - control,A: 9 sig (G, Q, L, R, W, K, I, S, Y); min p_adj = 9.89e-08
  - control,E: 4 sig (Y, K, M, W); min p_adj = 0.00358
  - control,P: 10 sig (G, M, Q, K, E, I, L, R, W, D); min p_adj = 5.67e-08

## Biological interpretation

**Day-10 control ribosomes have undergone a broad site-P composition reshuffling that BWM has not (concrete).** Control,P contributes 10/23 of its FDR<0.05 hits — including G↑ (OR=1.29, p_adj=5.7e-8), the C/R/E up-trio, and the M/W/Q/K/I/L down-band — and the corresponding control,P volcano panel is the most-populated single panel of the file. The mirror BWM,P panel has only 2 FDR<0.05 hits (E↑, K↓). The biology this anchors is more compositional than mechanistic: across a 10-day in-vivo culture, the unperturbed (control) ribosome population is sampling a different spectrum of P-site residues than at day 0, while the BWM-perturbed line is holding steadier at site P. Whether that reflects developmental drift in the wild-type animal across the timecourse, a shift in transcript composition, or an actual change in elongation kinetics is not resolvable from this file alone — but the magnitude of the control-side reshuffling sets a baseline against which the BWM-side numbers should be read.

**Lys at the E site flips direction between the two conditions, and both directions are FDR<0.01 (concrete).** Site-E K is BWM-depleted at day 10 vs day 0 (OR=0.79, p_adj=4.5e-5) but control-enriched at day 10 vs day 0 (OR=1.13, FDR<0.01 per Numbers at a glance). This is the file's clearest divergent-direction signature in BWM vs control: within BWM, day-10 ribosomes are *less* likely to have Lys exiting the E site than at day 0; within control, day-10 ribosomes are *more* likely. Lys is the canonical basic-residue exit-tunnel slowing residue (electrostatic interaction with rRNA backbone), so a divergent E-site Lys trajectory across the timecourse is consistent with the two conditions having different basic-residue traffic through the exit channel by day 10. Worth carrying forward to the codon-resolution sister CSV (does the K direction split localize to AAA or AAG?) and to the d5_vs_d0 contrast (is the d10 flip already present at d5, or does it emerge only by d10?).

**P-site Glu and P-site Lys are the two largest-magnitude shared-direction cells (concrete).** Both BWM,P,E↑ (OR=1.32, p_adj=2.4e-4) and control,P,E↑ (OR=1.16, FDR<0.01 per Numbers at a glance) move the same way: more Glu at the P site at day 10 than day 0 in both conditions. And both BWM,P,K↓ (OR=0.76, p_adj=5.5e-5) and the control,P depletion signature (containing K alongside M/W/Q/I/L) point the same way: less Lys at P at day 10 than day 0 in both conditions. Shared-direction effects in a within-condition contrast are the design's least confounded reading — they describe how each condition's day_10 stall composition differs from its own day_0 baseline in a way that the two conditions agree on, so condition-specific signals (e.g. the BWM perturbation) are not what's driving these particular rows. Most parsimonious mechanistic anchor: across the 10-day window in this organism, ribosomes are sampling a slightly different P-site residue distribution (Glu in, Lys out) regardless of the BWM/control axis.

**Site-A Asn enrichment in BWM at day 10 is the file's largest BWM-specific magnitude shift (exploratory).** BWM,A,N at OR=1.78 (p_adj=9.2e-7) is the largest |log₂(OR)| in the BWM column, and control,A,N is essentially flat (OR=0.86, p_adj=0.053 per the headline; not significant). On its face this would read as a BWM-specific gain of Asn at the A site by day 10, but the control,A panel is so heavily populated (9 FDR<0.05 hits including L↓, Q↓, W↓, S↓, Y↓, G↑, R↑) that the control side is *not* a flat reference — it is reshuffling in a different direction. So "BWM-specific Asn enrichment" is a candidate hypothesis rather than a finding: it could equally be (a) a real BWM-driven gain of Asn-at-A by day 10, or (b) a passive consequence of control losing Asn-at-A through composition redistribution that BWM did not undergo. Cross-checking against the codon-resolution sister CSV (does AAT or AAC carry the BWM,A,N signal at codon resolution?) and against the d10_vs_d5 aa contrast (does the Asn enrichment appear in the second half of the timecourse?) is the natural next step.

**The day-10-vs-day-0 difference between BWM and control hit counts (12 vs 23) is partly a power story (exploratory).** The 60-vs-60 test-count split is balanced, but if BWM and control have different total k counts in the underlying contingencies, the same effect size would produce different p magnitudes. The headline does not give the per-condition pooled totals for this file, so the 12-vs-23 gap is **not** decomposable here into "effect-size difference" vs "power difference" without reading the raw CSV — and Olive does not read raw stats CSVs. Treat the asymmetry as suggestive but not as evidence that "control reshuffles more than BWM"; carrying this question to the codon-resolution sister CSV and to Chumeng's synthesis layer is the appropriate next step.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the broad control,P composition reshuffling (G/C/R/E up vs M/W/Q/K/I/L down), the E-site Lys direction flip between BWM and control, and the shared-direction P:E↑ and P:K↓ across both conditions as design-clean "within-condition timecourse drift" signals. *Exploratory*: the BWM-specific A:N enrichment (needs codon and d10_vs_d5 corroboration) and the BWM-vs-control hit-count asymmetry (needs per-condition pooled totals to resolve). *Noise*: rare-aa flagged rows (BWM,E,C; control,A,W; control,P,M; control,P,W; control,E,W) — the OR magnitudes are unstable at low day_10_count and large |log₂(OR)| may reflect sampling rather than biology.

## Methods
Dylan proposed Fisher's exact (two-sided) on a 2×2 of (feature_count, total − feature_count) at each timepoint within one condition; user confirmed. The script (`stall_sites_non_consensus_stats.py`) computes BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 tests for AA-level (so each of the 6 sub-families is corrected independently, not against the full 120). The test does **not** answer whether the same feature is changing in BWM and control in the same direction — that comparison is the user's job (see "control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction" caveat) and is the largest-magnitude reading from this file's cells. Counts (`day_X_count`) and totals (`day_X_total`) are summed across replicates before the test, hence the family-level pseudoreplication caveat.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** 2×2 contingencies pool replicates by design; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative — read `odds_ratio` as the primary effect column and treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as evidence of additional certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** Pooled (day_10 + day_0) totals at the condition level are in the thousands (6091, 6945, 8788, 27732 across cells); even tiny relative deviations can drive `p_adj` to 1e-7 or below — rank by `odds_ratio` (or log₂ of it) when ordering effects within a (condition, site) family.
- **bh-per-(condition,site).** BH is run independently within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs; two cells with the same raw p in different (condition, site) families can land at different `p_adj` — rank within a (condition, site) cell, not across the full 120-test grid.
- **within-condition-clean.** No condition pooling and no timepoint pooling; the file structurally answers "does day_10 differ from day_0 *within* this condition" cleanly, leaving the BWM-vs-control comparison to be done by reading paired panels side-by-side rather than by the test itself.
- **rare-aa-low-count.** Rare AAs (W, M, C and several others by cell) carry `day_X_count` below 100 in BWM and below 200 in control across some (condition, site) cells (e.g. BWM,P,W with day_10=36, day_0=38). Rank by OR but flag magnitude as unstable for these rows.
- **OR-direction-asymmetry.** No zero-cell rows in this file, so no infinite/zero ORs; the caveat is preserved as a ranking-axis discipline note — use log₂(OR), not OR, when ordering effects so the positive/negative bands are symmetric.
- **control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction.** Three site×aa cells show BWM and control going opposite ways or one flat while the other moves: site-A N (BWM↑1.78 FDR<1e-6 vs control 0.86 ns), site-E K (BWM↓0.79 vs control↑1.13, both FDR<0.01 in opposite directions), site-P G (BWM 1.01 ns vs control↑1.28 FDR<1e-7). These are the design's *target* observations and should anchor the BWM-specific reading at Chumeng's synthesis layer.

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM A-site amino-acid volcano: log₂(OR) on x, −log₁₀(FDR) on y; triangles are FDR<0.05. Asparagine (N, polar purple) at log₂(OR) ≈ +0.83 sits as the far-right outlier and highest single point in the panel; leucine (L, hydrophobic green), tyrosine (Y, hydrophobic green), and alanine (A, hydrophobic green) are FDR<0.05 depleted at log₂(OR) ≈ −0.35 to −0.45; isoleucine (I, green) and phenylalanine (F, green) are modestly enriched. All FDR<0.05 hits except N sit within the ±0.5 log₂(OR) band.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/BWM_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM P-site amino-acid volcano: only 2 FDR<0.05 hits — lysine (K, basic blue) at log₂(OR) ≈ −0.40 (depleted) and glutamate (E, acidic red) at log₂(OR) ≈ +0.40 (enriched). All other amino acids collapse to a tight cluster near the origin; this is structurally the sparsest BWM panel of the row.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/BWM_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM E-site amino-acid volcano: lysine (K, basic blue) at log₂(OR) ≈ −0.34 sits highest on the y-axis as the single depleted FDR<0.05 hit; on the positive side, glutamine (Q, polar purple), serine (S, polar purple), and cysteine (C, polar purple) are FDR<0.05 enriched at log₂(OR) ≈ +0.5 to +0.85, with C farthest right.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/BWM_E_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control A-site amino-acid volcano: 9 FDR<0.05 hits clustered across the ±0.5 log₂(OR) band — glutamine (Q, polar purple) and glycine (G, neutral orange) at log₂(OR) ≈ −0.6 and +0.36 sit at the top of the y-axis (−log₁₀(FDR) ≈ 7); leucine (L, green) and arginine (R, basic blue) are FDR<0.05 modestly displaced (~−0.34 and +0.34); tryptophan (W, green) is at log₂(OR) ≈ −0.6; lysine (K, blue), serine (S, polar), tyrosine (Y, green), and isoleucine (I, green) are also FDR<0.05 with smaller magnitudes.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/control_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control P-site amino-acid volcano: the most-populated panel of the file — 10 FDR<0.05 hits. Glycine (G, neutral orange) at log₂(OR) ≈ +0.36 sits highest (−log₁₀(FDR) ≈ 7.2); methionine (M, hydrophobic green) at log₂(OR) ≈ −0.68 and glutamine (Q, polar purple) at ≈ −0.45 are the strongest depletions; lysine (K, basic blue), isoleucine (I, green), leucine (L, green), and tryptophan (W, green) are FDR<0.05 depleted within the ±0.5 log₂(OR) band; glutamate (E, acidic red), arginine (R, basic blue), aspartate (D, red), and cysteine (C, polar purple) are FDR<0.05 enriched.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/control_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control E-site amino-acid volcano: a near-null panel — 4 FDR<0.05 hits, all within the ±0.5 log₂(OR) band. Tyrosine (Y, hydrophobic green) and methionine (M, green) are depleted (log₂(OR) ≈ −0.45 and −0.40); tryptophan (W, green) sits at the FDR threshold at log₂(OR) ≈ −0.55 (rare-aa flagged in Top hits); lysine (K, basic blue) is the lone FDR<0.05 enriched hit at log₂(OR) ≈ +0.18.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/control_E_volcano.png)
